behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels for attendee attribution in summaries.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): model-output. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): model-output->Agentic. Speaker diarization correctness depends on real STT model output; stubs can hide label-loss failures at the STT->LLM handoff. Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets a single quality boundary: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels" (not a multi-agentic chain assertion).
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the real STT API and assert labeled speakers are present and preserved in the payload consumed by summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): local-logic. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): local-logic->Unit. This is deterministic parsing/transformation logic and can fail within one function/object boundary. Rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically "Transcript parsing extracts... into the summary input structure" and does not require real module wiring, OS, or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments, assert parser output includes exact assignee and task text fields in the input schema.

behaviors.description: Live summary contains at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): model-output. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): model-output->Agentic. Coverage of assigned attendees in generated action items depends on real LLM judgment/content, which mocks can overfit and miss. Rejected Workflow because this assertion is about one output-quality criterion in the produced summary, not explicit validation of multiple agentic stage interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real meeting transcript/audio with known per-attendee assignments, call the real summarizer and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into `decisions` schema section, not generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): module-handoff. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): module-handoff->Integration. The failure boundary is extractor output handoff into schema-mapping/writer modules. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no real AI output required because decisions are already "identified"; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected Agentic due behavior text "routes identified decisions" disqualifying dependence on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire decision-extraction output module to summary-schema assembler in-process; feed identified decisions and assert they land only in `decisions` fields, never narrative slots.

behaviors.description: Key transcript decisions appear in live summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): model-output. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): model-output->Agentic. Correctness depends on real model extraction/placement quality for decisions in generated output. Rejected Workflow because behavior text checks a single summary quality outcome, not explicit correctness across multiple agentic handoff stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript content with known decisions through the real LLM summarization path and assert those decisions appear in the `decisions` section (and are absent from narrative-only placement).

behaviors.description: For audio longer than 60 minutes, service segments input, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): multi-step-agentic. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): multi-step-agentic->Workflow. This behavior spans chained segment processing + merge where correctness depends on multiple model calls and cross-step context preservation; lower-level mocks can miss coherence/regression across segment boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute meeting recording; verify segment splitting boundaries, sequential processing across all segments, and final merged summary coherence (no dropped/duplicated major items across segment joins).

behaviors.description: End-to-end pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, structured delivery) completes automatically for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): multi-step-agentic. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): multi-step-agentic->Workflow. This is an explicit full pipeline success criterion with multiple staged operations including model-dependent steps; only workflow-level execution catches orchestration and stage-to-stage failures without manual intervention.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end run on a real meeting audio file and assert autonomous completion plus valid structured output containing expected top-level sections.
